attributes.description: Alerts when MQPUT, MQPUT1 or MQGET failures occur. Put/get failures indicate queue-full, auth, or application errors.
attributes.name: [IBM MQ OTel] Failed message operations

investigation_guide.blob:
## IBM MQ failed message operations

### What fired
Rate of `failed_mqput_total + failed_mqput1_total + failed_mqget_total` > 0 during the window.

### Why it matters
MQ tracks every put/get failure. In a healthy system these should be zero. Elevated failures indicate queue full, authorization issues, or misbehaving applications.

### Triage
1. Split by queue to find hot spots.
2. Check queue depth vs MAXDEPTH.
3. Inspect application logs for reason codes.

### Remediation
- Increase queue depth or consumer capacity.
- Fix authorization problems.

### Tuning
- Fires on any increase.
